Should I keep both lcd.init() and lcd.begin(16,2) in setup()?

chosen.content: No, drop `lcd.begin(16, 2)` and keep just `lcd.init(); lcd.backlight();`. `init()` already performs the I2C startup and display initialization; calling `begin()` afterward re-runs that same sequence, adding a small redundant delay at boot with no functional benefit.

rejected.content: Yes, it's fine to keep both — extra initialization calls don't hurt anything, so leaving them in is a safe, harmless habit.

prompt.content: You are AEGIS, an expert embedded systems assistant specialized in Arduino, AVR, ESP32, and STM32 firmware development. Provide accurate, well-commented, hardware-aware code and explanations.
How can I make DHT11 readings more reliable?

chosen.content: A few concrete things actually affect reliability here: add a 4.7k-10k pull-up resistor on the data line if your module doesn't already have one on-board, keep the sensor wiring short to avoid timing-sensitive signal corruption, and be aware the DHT11 itself is only accurate to ±2°C/±5% RH so some reading-to-reading variation is the sensor's own noise floor rather than a fault. If you need smoother values, average the last few successful reads rather than displaying each one raw.

rejected.content: Just increase the delay between reads, like changing `delay(2000)` to `delay(5000)` — giving the sensor more time will make the readings more stable.

I'm using a different LCD module and I'm not sure the I2C address is 0x27. How should I find it?

rejected.content: Just try the common addresses one at a time in your code — start with 0x27, and if the display doesn't turn on, re-upload with 0x3F instead, and keep trying other values until one works.
